cases.title: 1. Asia (The Giant)
cases.description: The largest continent in the world, covering 30% of Earth's land area.
steps: Size: 1st Largest (approx 44.5 million km²).
Key Boundary: Separated from Africa by the Red Sea and the Suez Canal.
Key Boundary: Separated from North America by the Bering Strait.
PLE Fact: It is the most populated continent (China & India have the highest populations).
Economy: Historically known for the trade of Silk and Spices.

cases.title: 2. Africa (Our Home)
cases.description: The most central continent, often called the 'Cradle of Mankind'.
steps: Size: 2nd Largest (approx 30.3 million km²).
Position: Crossed by the Equator, Tropic of Cancer, and Tropic of Capricorn.
Water Borders: Mediterranean Sea (North), Red Sea (North-East), Indian Ocean (East), Atlantic Ocean (West).
Strategic Point: Separated from Europe by the Strait of Gibraltar (only 14km apart).
Extreme Points: Ras Ben Sakka (North), Cape Agulhas (South), Cape Guardafui (East), Cape Verde (West).

cases.title: 3. North America
cases.description: The continent found entirely in the Northern Hemisphere.
steps: Size: 3rd Largest continent.
Key Boundary: Separated from South America by the Panama Canal (a man-made waterway).
Major Countries: USA, Canada, and Mexico.
Discovery: Christopher Columbus is often credited with 'discovering' it for Europe in 1492 (The New World).
Economy: Highly industrialized and advanced in technology.

cases.title: 4. South America
cases.description: The continent with the largest rainforest (The Amazon).
steps: Size: 4th Largest continent.
Geography: Crossed by the Equator and the Tropic of Capricorn.
Key Feature: The Amazon Rainforest is called the 'Lungs of the Earth'.
Economy: Famous for coffee growing (Brazil is the leading producer).
Rivers: Home to the Amazon River (Largest volume of water in the world).

cases.title: 5. Antarctica
cases.description: The frozen continent located at the South Pole.
steps: Size: 5th Largest continent.
Climate: It is a cold desert. It is the coldest, driest, and windiest continent.
Population: It has no permanent human population. Only scientists live there temporarily for research.
PLE Fact: It is not a country, but a continent governed by international treaty.

cases.title: 6. Europe
cases.description: Attached to Asia (forming Eurasia), but politically distinct.
steps: Size: 6th Largest (Second smallest).
Key Feature: The Prime Meridian (0° longitude) passes through London (Greenwich).
History: The source of colonialists (British, French, Germans) who partitioned Africa.
Geography: Separated from Asia by the Ural Mountains and the Caspian Sea.

cases.title: 7. Australia (Oceania)
cases.description: The smallest continent, lying entirely in the Southern Hemisphere.
steps: Size: Smallest continent.
Type: It is an 'Island Continent' because it is surrounded by water on all sides.
Geography: Located between the Indian Ocean and the Pacific Ocean.
Economy: Famous for Merino Sheep rearing (Wool production).
PLE Fact: It is the only continent that is also a single country.
